tasmania-guy-barnett-lyons.summary: Stated position / federal motion: As a Liberal Senator for Tasmania, in June 2008 Guy Barnett moved a motion in the Senate to end Medicare funding for abortions occurring after the second trimester, stating he wanted to show "compassion and care for the unborn... rather than killing them in many cases for psychosocial reasons, which is effectively abortion on request."

sources.title: Brad Battin

victoria-ellen-sandell-melbourne.summary: Sandell, leader of the Victorian Greens, has publicly supported entrenching abortion access in the Victorian Constitution. In a 4 February 2025 Greens statement supporting the Constitution Amendment (Abortion) Bill 2024 she said 'People deserve to know where their leaders sit when it comes to their reproductive rights and access to essential healthcare,' and warned it was 'frightening that all it takes is for a change of government to threaten our right to safely access abortion in Victoria, especially when the Liberals are lurching further to the right here.'

sources.title: Vic Greens push to protect abortion from renewed conservative threat

victoria-georgie-crozier-southern-metrop.summary: Crozier (Liberal, Shadow Health Minister) said of a 2022 crossbench bill on conscientious objection by religiously affiliated hospitals that she had not yet seen the legislation, stating 'I need to see the details in the bill. I'm very supportive of women's rights.' In February 2025 debate she characterised the Greens' Constitution Amendment (Abortion) Bill 2024 to entrench abortion in the constitution as a political manoeuvre rather than genuinely protecting abortion from politicisation.

sources.title: Victorian crossbench MP launches bid to force religiously affiliated hospitals to perform abortions

victoria-georgie-purcell-northern-victor.summary: Purcell (Animal Justice Party) is a vocal advocate for expanded abortion access. In a June 2022 Guardian opinion piece she argued nurses should be permitted to prescribe medical abortions, and in Parliament has spoken in favour of expanding abortion services in rural and regional Victoria, drawing on her own experience of difficulties accessing services and harassment before safe access zones. In October 2025 she co-authored, with Rachel Payne, a report calling for free medical and surgical abortion for all Victorians, expanded public-hospital provision, and extending medical abortion access from nine to twelve weeks.

sources.title: Georgie Purcell
Access in Action - Purcell and Payne launch bold push to reform abortion in Victoria

victoria-jacinta-allan-bendigo-east.summary: Allan, member for Bendigo East since 1999, voted for the Abortion Law Reform Act 2008 that decriminalised abortion in Victoria; her Wikipedia biography notes she received abusive emails and a picket of her electorate office by anti-abortion campaigners over the vote and was targeted by Right to Life groups in the 2010 campaign. As Premier in October 2024 she said there are 'real and genuine threats to the protections women have fought for and won' and that legislation to protect those rights 'is being looked at being torn down at too many opportunities by conservative politicians.'

sources.title: Jacinta Allan
We must keep fighting for abortion rights, Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan says

victoria-john-pesutto-hawthorn.summary: Pesutto (Liberal), Opposition Leader until December 2024, expressed personal support for abortion and said he would put the issue to a conscience vote, as noted in a 4 February 2025 Victorian Greens statement describing the recent Liberal leadership change.

sources.title: Vic Greens push to protect abortion from renewed conservative threat

victoria-lily-d-ambrosio-mill-park.summary: D'Ambrosio, member for Mill Park since 2002 (and thus in Parliament for the 2008 reform), has publicly supported expanding abortion access. In a 29 November 2023 statement marking the 15th anniversary of Victoria's 2008 decriminalisation of abortion, she promoted the Allan Labor Government's expansion of surgical termination services across Melbourne and new sexual and reproductive health hubs, noting safe access zones around abortion providers had been legislated.

sources.title: Improving Abortion Access Across Melbourne

victoria-sarah-mansfield-western-victori.summary: Mansfield (Australian Greens, health spokesperson) introduced the Constitution Amendment (Abortion) Bill 2024 into the Legislative Council on 12 November 2024, which sought to entrench the Abortion Law Reform Act 2008 in the Victorian Constitution so it could only be altered by a three-fifths supermajority. Seconding the bill on 4 February 2025 she said 'Abortion is already a postcode lottery in Victoria, we can't risk a change in government further threatening access to this essential healthcare' and 'If there was ever a time to do this, it's now.'
